rulings.Case Title: CACV OF COLORADO, LLC VS. WAYNE WONG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Application For Order/Judgment For Sale Of Dwelling; Declaration Of Edward T. Weber; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof; And Request For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, January 9, 2015, Line 1, ASSIGNEE GRYPHON SOLUTIONS, LLC Motion For Application For Order/Judgment For Sale Of Dwelling; Declaration Of Edward T. Weber; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof; And Request For Judicial Notice. In reliance on the evidence provided by the Petitioner, the order for sale should not issue. Based on the Petitioner's representations, the Court has determined that the property is subject to a homestead exemption in the amount $50,000. (CCP §§ 703.050, 703.100.) The Court accepts the appraised value of $625,000 for the property, and determines that a one-third interest is subject to sale for satisfaction of Petitioner's judgment held against Respondent. The Litigation Guarantee provided by Petitioner indicates a Deed of Trust on the property in the amount of $186,500. Thus, the net equity in the property amounts to -$28,166.67, which is insufficient for the Court to order a sale of the property. (CCP § 704.780(b).) Petitioner's Application indicates that the amount of the Deed of Trust was reported on October 10, 2014 to be $117,887 but Petitioner provided no support for this assertion. Petitioner is ordered to provide, if it can, evidence sufficient to support the reduced Deed of Trust amount by 9:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing for the Court's consideration prior to issuing a final order on this Application. If no evidence is received, the Application will be denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: FEI SU VS. SYNERGY PROJECT MANAGEMENT, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For A Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, January 9, 2015, Line 1, PLAINTIFF FEI SU'S Motion For A Protective Order Pro Tem Judge Vedica Puri, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: CHUCK AMITAL, SUCCESSOR TRUSTEE OF THE FRED S. VS. NORMANDY APARTMENTS, A CALIFORNIA GENERAL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Compliance With Court'S October 24, 2014 Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, January 9, 2015, Line 2, PLAINTIFF CHUCK AMITAL, SUCCESSOR TRUSTEE OF THE FRED S. ISAK AND ROSE R. ISAK TRUST'S MOTION To Compel Compliance With Court'S October 24, 2014 Order Pro Tem Judge Vedica Puri, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Court is unable to determine precisely what discovery has been provided in compliance with the prior October 24, 2104 Order. To that extent, a hearing is required. However, it is clear that the ordered discovery that has been provided was untimely. Given the facts as described in Mr. Ericksen's declaration, sanctions for failing to comply with the October 24, 2104 order shall be granted in the amount of $2700, the amount of attorney's fees in connection with the second motion to compel. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: FIRST MERCURY INSURANCE COMPANY, A CORPORATION VS. LAW OFFICES OF BORNSTEIN & BORNSTEIN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order To Compel Answers To Interrogatories And Responses To Demand For Inspection Of Documents And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, January 9, 2015, Line 3, PLAINTIFF FIRST MERCURY INSURANCE COMPANY, A CORPORATION'S MOTION For Order To Compel Answers To Interrogatories And Responses To Demand For Inspection Of Documents And For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Vedica Puri, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to vpuri@pillsburycoleman.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: DESOTO CAB COMPANY, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. DARNICE LINTON
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Order To Compel Compliance With Deposition Notice And Document Requests To Greg Cochran; Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, January 9, 2015, Line 15, RESPONDENT DARNICE LINTON Motion For Order To Compel Compliance With Deposition Notice And Document Requests To Greg Cochran; Request For Sanctions. Denied. The evidence shows that the witness was not produced to due miscommunication from busy schedules of both counsel. (See Seibert Decl., Exs. C-O.) Defense counsel attempted to comply with her obligations under the Discovery Act by offering dates to produce the witnesses within the week or two after November 13 and 14. (Id., Ex. Q.) This issue could have been resolved through meet and confer efforts. Plaintiff's counsel shall pay Defendant sanctions in the amount of $500.00 within 30 days of entry of this Order. (See CCP sec. 2023.030(a).) The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
